appSubtitle: View and manage your filament spools
rfidNotFound: RFID scanned: {{uid}} — spool not found in current inventory.
td1sNotFound: TD1S scan: {{td}} — spool not found in inventory.
stgDataHelp: Export your inventory locally as JSON, or copy the public API URL (with your access key embedded) so a script or another machine can fetch it remotely.
stgGroupInvSub: Collapse identical spools into a single entry
stgCopyApiUrlOk: Copied — keep this URL private; anyone with it can read your inventory.
stgCopyApiUrlNoKey: Access key not loaded yet — try again in a moment.
addAccountAuthError: Invalid email or password. Please check your credentials.
confirmDeleteAccount: Remove this account and all its local data?
delModalWarn: This account leaves this device — nothing permanent, come back whenever.
addProductSub: Same fields a TigerTag chip carries — saved to the cloud now, ready for chip programming later.
addProductErrMissing: Please pick a brand and material.
addProductErrCapacity: Capacity must be at least 1 g.
addProductErrAspectSame: Aspect 1 and Aspect 2 must be different.
noInventory: Nothing to chew on here… yet.
noMatch: No spool matches your search. They hide well.
rawScaleHint: Weigh spool + container on the scale — the server subtracts the container weight.
twinLinkPickerSub: Same brand, material, type and colour as this spool.
upgradeToPlusHelpTip: How to find the product ID
toolDuplicateTip: Clone as a new TigerCloud spool (hold to confirm)
loginResetSent: Check your inbox — reset link sent!
loginPasswordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
avatarTooLarge: Image too large (max 500 KB after resize)
tdEditConnectMsg: Connect your TD1S sensor via USB and remove filament
tdConnectMsg: Connect your TD1S sensor via USB
invEmptyTitle: Time to scan your filament spools!
invEmptyText: Grab the TigerTag app, scan your spools and watch your inventory come to life. It takes 10 seconds per spool — we timed it.
setupNameSub: Your display name is visible in the app and to your friends!
invWelcomeTitle: Scan & manage your inventory & 3D printers from your phone.
invWelcomeSub: Scan your TigerTag spools in 10 seconds. Your inventory updates in real time.
invQrScanHint: Scan QR code to install on your mobile phone
notifsEmptySub: Friend requests and updates show up here
friendNotFound: No user found with this code.
friendRegenConfirm: Regenerate private key? Current friends keep their access. Old links are revoked.
friendsEmptySub: Add someone with their code and snoop through their stash.
friendInvErrorHint: Your friend may have changed their access key. Ask them to remove and re-add you, or to enable public mode.
friendNotSharing: This user does not wish to share their inventory.
racksEmptySub: Create a rack to organise your spool storage.
racksEmptyFriendSub: This user hasn't set up any racks yet.
rackEditSub: Pick a preset or create a custom layout.
rackDeleteConfirm: Delete the rack "{{name}}"? Spools assigned to it will be unassigned.
rackUnrankedEmptyHint: Spools that aren't in a rack are kept here.
rackUnstoreLockedTip: Empty spool — Auto Unstorage would send it straight back here, so it can't be stored.
rackAutoStoreNoDrop: Drop here to keep it unstored (turns off Auto storage)
printersEmptyBullet1: Bambu Lab, Creality, Elegoo, FlashForge or Snapmaker
printersEmptyBullet2: Sync over your local network — credentials never leave your account
printersEmptyBullet3: Pick the active printer for each spool when you scan a tag
printersFriendNASub: A friend's 3D printers are owner-only — they're not visible from this view.
printerAddPickSub: Choose the manufacturer of your 3D printer
snapNoConnection: Open this view while the printer is on your network to see live status.
acuMotorsOff: Disable Motors, Click to Manually Move the Axis
snapFilUnloadEmpty: No extruders have filament to unload.
snapLogEmpty: No traffic yet — open the panel while the printer is online.
snapFilEditableTip: Click to edit this filament manually
snapFilLockedTip: Read-only — RFID tag detected by the printer
snapFilEditError: Send failed — check the printer's WebSocket.
printerAddErrName: Please give your printer a name.
printerAddErrSave: Couldn't save. Check your network and try again.
printerHelperBambuBullets: Open Settings → General → LAN Only Mode on your printer.
Note the IP, Serial Number and 8-digit Access Code shown on screen.
TigerTag connects over MQTT on your local network.
printerHelperCrealityBullets: Find the IP in Network → Printer Information.
Use your Klipper / Moonraker access password.
printerHelperElegooBullets: Find your IP in the printer's network settings. Serial number is on the printer label or Settings menu.
printerHelperFFGBullets: Find the IP and Serial in About → Network.
Use the password you set in FlashPrint or Orca-Flashforge.
printerHelperSnapBullets: Just enter the local IP shown on the Snapmaker network screen.
printerHintBambuIP: Local IP shown in LAN Only Mode.
printerHintBambuCode: 8-digit code from the LAN Only Mode screen.
printerHintBambuSerial: Listed below the access code on the same screen.
printerHintElegooSerial: Serial number from the printer's About screen.
printerHintElegooMqtt: Access code from the printer's network settings (default: 123456).
printerHintFFGSerial: Serial number from the printer's About screen.
printerHintFFGPassword: Same password you use in FlashPrint.
printerHintSnapIP: Local IP shown on the Snapmaker network screen.
snapScanEmpty: No Snapmaker found. Check it's powered on and on the same Wi-Fi.
snapAddChoiceSub: How do you want to find your printer?
snapAddChoiceManualHint: Manually enter the printer's local IP
ffgScanEmpty: No FlashForge printers found on your LAN
creScanEmpty: No Creality printers found on your LAN
bambuScanEmpty: No Bambu Lab printers found on your LAN
elegooScanEmpty: No Elegoo printers found on your LAN
snapManualSub: Type the printer's local IP — we'll probe it to pre-fill the rest.
snapScanNoSubnets: No network detected. Connect to Wi-Fi.
snapScanExtraSubnetsHint: Add another network if your printer is elsewhere (e.g. 192.168.40).
snapScanEmptyFirewall: No reply on {{p}}. Use "Add by IP" with the exact address.
printerSecretWarn: These secrets grant LAN-level access to your printer. Don't share them or paste into untrusted apps.
errReportSub: Copy this report and send it to support so we can help.
rackAutoFillTip: Place unranked spools into empty unlocked slots
rackLockedTip: Locked · right-click a slot to lock/unlock
storageAutoAssignTip: Place in the first available slot
autoUnstorageSub: Free the rack slot when a spool reaches 0g
autoOrganizeSub: Auto-places new spools and frees the slot when a spool runs out. Lock a slot for an exception.
rackStatsDepletedTip: Active spools with no filament left (weight 0g)
dbgDeletedPurgeConfirm: Permanently delete "{{name}}" from the database? This cannot be undone.
dbgDeletedPurgeConfirmTwin: Permanently delete "{{name}}" AND its linked twin tag from the database? This cannot be undone.
aboutAutoUpdateSub: Check for new versions on startup
rackEmptyConfirm: Empty rack "{{name}}"? All assigned spools will be unassigned (the rack itself stays).
scaleRemoveConfirm: Remove "{{name}}" from the list? It will reappear automatically on next heartbeat.
scaleEmptyTitle: Add a TigerScale to your setup
scaleEmptySub: Place a spool on the scale — it's automatically detected, weighed, and synced with your inventory. Zero clicks.
scaleEmptyBullet1: Automatically detects the RFID and identifies your spool
scaleEmptyBullet2: Subtracts the container weight so you see how much filament is really available
scaleEmptyBullet3: Fully hands-free — no app to open, no button to press
rackClearAllConfirm: Clear all spools from "{{name}}"? They will be returned to "Unranked spools" (the rack stays).
spoolMarkDeletedTip: Hold 1.5s to remove this spool from your inventory (it will appear in Settings → Debug → Deleted, restorable from there)
uidMigrConfirmMsg: {{count}} spools in your inventory use the legacy storage format. Upgrading converts them to the new format. Estimated time: about {{duration}} — please do not close the app while it's running.
uidMigrProgressMsg: Your inventory is being upgraded to a new, more efficient storage format. Please do not close the app until this finishes. Each spool only takes a fraction of a second.
uidMigrProgressWarn: ⚠ Closing the app now would just resume on next launch — but for the cleanest experience, let it finish.
rackMigrConfirmMsg: We found {{count}} spool(s) using the legacy storage layout. Upgrading regroups the rack location into a single tidy field — takes {{duration}}, runs in the background, and your spools stay where they are.
rackMigrProgressMsg: Your storage assignments are being repackaged. Please don't close the app — each spool only takes a fraction of a second.
rackMigrProgressWarn: ⚠ Closing the app now would just resume on next launch — but for the cleanest experience, let it finish.
migrationConfirmMsg: A small data update is ready for {{count}} item(s). It runs in the background and takes {{duration}}. Your data stays exactly where it is — nothing will move or get lost.
uidMigrConfirmMobileWarn: Before continuing, make sure your mobile app is updated to version 1.0.3 or later.
migrationProgressMsg: Tidying up your data — please don't close the app. Each item only takes a fraction of a second.
migrationProgressWarn: ⚠ Closing now would just resume on next launch — but for the cleanest experience, let it finish.
ffgErrSnMismatch: Bad serial number — please check printer settings
ffgErrBadPassword: Bad password — please check printer settings
ffgCamFailMsg: Camera connection failed — only one viewer is allowed at a time.
ffgErrNetwork: Cannot reach the printer — check IP and network.
bblRtspHint: Not supported natively — open in VLC or another media player
rackNoSpaceHint: No free space — add a rack to store the remaining spools.
tigerPodModalDesc: A 3D-printable stand to Burn TigerTag RFID chips directly on your filament spools.
upgradeToPlusHelpBody: The product ID is the number shown in the URL of a TigerTag product page. Browse the material list, open any product, and copy the number at the end of the URL.
encModalSub: Place your chip on the reader, then burn.
encPlaceChips: Hold the RFID tags in front of the readers
encBurning: Writing… keep the chip(s) on the reader(s).
encSameUid: The two readers see the same chip — use two distinct chips.
encOverwriteWarn: A chip already contains data — it will be erased.
camWallEmptySub: Add a printer with a camera to see live feeds here.
tutoNone: No connection tutorial is available for this printer yet.
tutoStuckLink: I'm stuck — open the official guide
tutoBambuX1Step1: Step 1: Enable LAN Only mode on the printer side. Enter the Settings page from the left side of the printer screen and click Settings.
tutoBambuX1Step4: Choose whether to enable LAN mode liveview according to your needs.
tutoBambuX1Step5: Step 2: Enable Developer mode on the printer side. Enable "Developer Mode".
tutoBambuX1Step6: Please carefully read the Important Notice and Risk warning. If you accept all related risks or consequences, check "I confirm reading and understanding the Important Notice above" and then select "Enable Developer Mode".
tutoBambuX1Step7: Once this feature is enabled, the button will turn green. Then use the printer IP address, Serial Number and Access Code in Tiger Studio.
tutoBambuP1Step1: Step 1: Enable LAN Only mode on the printer side. Enter the Settings page and select WLAN.
tutoBambuP1Step3: Select Yes, and enable LAN Only mode.
tutoBambuP1Step4: After enabling LAN Only mode, ON will be displayed.
tutoBambuP1Step5: Step 2: Enable Developer mode on the printer side. After turning on LAN Only mode, click the Scroll Down button and find Developer Mode.
tutoBambuP1Step6: Select Developer Mode, and please read the Important Notice carefully.
tutoBambuP1Step7: If you accept all related risks or consequences, scroll down to the end and click Enable.
tutoBambuP1Step8: After enabling Developer Mode, ON will be displayed. Then use the printer IP address, Serial Number and Access Code in Tiger Studio.
tutoBambuA1Step1: Step 1: Enable LAN Only Mode on the printer side. Enter the Setting page from the printer screen by clicking Setting.
tutoBambuA1Step4: Step 2: Enable Developer Mode on the printer side. Enable "Developer Mode".
tutoBambuA1Step5: Please carefully read the Important Notice. If you accept all related risks or consequences, check "I confirm reading and understanding the Important Notice above" and then select "Enable".
tutoBambuA1Step6: Once this feature is enabled, the button will turn green.
tutoBambuA1Step7: Check the printer IP address and Serial Number from the printer settings.
tutoBambuA1Step8: Use the printer IP address, Serial Number and Access Code in Tiger Studio.
tutoFlashforgeAd5xStep1: Step 1: On the main interface, tap the "Basic Info" button at the bottom right of the screen.
tutoFlashforgeAd5xStep2: Step 2: In the Basic Info page, retrieve the Serial No. and the IP Address.
tutoFlashforgeAd5xStep3: Step 3: Select the gear icon, tap "Network Mode", then retrieve the Printer ID.
tutoElegooStep1: On the touch screen, select Settings → LAN Only. Enable LAN Only + Access Code and select Confirm.
anycubicScanEmpty: No Anycubic printers found — make sure LAN mode is enabled on the printer
acuImportSub: Paired LAN printers found in the slicer's configuration
acuImportErrNotFound: AnycubicSlicerNext config not found. Install the slicer and pair your printer (LAN mode) once.
acuImportErrNoPrinters: No paired LAN printers in the slicer config. Open AnycubicSlicerNext and pair the printer in LAN mode once.
acuImportErrBridge: Connection bridge missing — fully quit and relaunch the app.
acuNoLayout: Connected — waiting for the ACE layout…
printerHintAnycubicIP: Printer's local IP — LAN mode must be ON
printerHintAnycubicModelId: Numeric model id (e.g. 20027) — filled by the slicer import
printerHintAnycubicDeviceId: 32-character device id — filled by the slicer import
printerHintAnycubicUsername: Broker username — filled by the slicer import
printerHintAnycubicPassword: Broker password — filled by the slicer import
printerHelperAnycubicBullets: Pair the printer once in AnycubicSlicerNext (LAN mode ON).
Use 'Import from Anycubic Slicer' to fill all fields automatically.
Credentials are durable — the slicer is not needed afterwards.
acuCloudChoiceHint: For printers in cloud mode (via the slicer)
acuCloudSub: Cloud printers on your Anycubic account
acuCloudHelpIntro: Cloud printers are added through AnycubicSlicerNext. Start it with remote debugging, signed in, and open the Workbench:
acuCloudErrUnreachable: Slicer not reachable. Launch AnycubicSlicerNext in bridge mode (below), signed in, with the Workbench open.
acuCloudErrNoWorkbench: Workbench not found. Open the Workbench tab in the slicer and sign in.
acuCloudErrNoToken: Not signed in. Sign in to your Anycubic account in the slicer, then retry.
acuCloudErrRest: Couldn't reach the Anycubic cloud. Check your connection and retry.
acuCloudErrNoPrinters: No cloud printers on your account.
acuCloudTokenStale: Cloud sign-in expired — re-add the printer with the slicer in bridge mode.
whatsNewReleaseTitle: Tiger Studio Manager {{v}} Public Release Note
whatsNewEmpty: No release notes for this version.
avatarPromptBody: Right now you're just a letter in a circle — thrilling, we know. Pick an avatar that's unmistakably you, so friends spot you at a glance and your shared inventory finally has some character. It takes ten seconds.
notifAvatarText: You're one of a kind — give yourself an avatar to match.
manageAccountsSub: Switch, sign in, sign out, have a blast.
friendRemoveConfirm: Do you really want to remove {{name}} from your friends?
